title: The Kevin Hart Fiasco and the Best Performances of 2018 With Wesley Morris | The Oscars Show (Ep. 106)
format: Sean and Amanda break down Kevin Hart stepping down as Oscars host and where Roma, A Star Is Born, and Green Book stand after the critics' awards. Then the New York Times' Wesley Morris joins to run through the year's best performances.

segments.heading: The Kevin Hart Fiasco
segments.note: and where the awards race stands after the critics' groups

films.title: Roma
films.note: swept the LA and New York film critics' best-film prizes; Amanda calls it the movie critics feel they have to protect and boost, which she thinks puts it in a tricky spot with the Academy.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: still quiet ahead of the Golden Globes, where 'Shallow' is up for a Grammy and a Globe; floated as this year's version of a movie critics resent but the Academy loves.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: the other contender Sean floats for that same slot.

films.title: The Favourite
films.note: expanding into 1,000 theaters the following weekend.

films.title: Widows
films.note: Steve McQueen's heist thriller; Daniel Kaluuya, Viola Davis, Cynthia Erivo, Elizabeth Debicki, and Brian Tyree Henry all get singled out.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Kaluuya's part is called too thin to show what he can really do.

films.title: If Beale Street Could Talk
films.note: Brian Tyree Henry is on screen for about 12 minutes and, Wesley says, the movie basically stops and revolves around him.

films.title: Boy Erased
films.note: Lucas Hedges is compelling even when the movie around him doesn't fully land, per Wesley.

films.title: Ben Is Back
films.note: Hedges and Julia Roberts' scenes together, especially the back half in the car, are the movie's strongest stretch.

films.title: Manchester by the Sea
films.note: the earlier Hedges performance they use as the yardstick.

films.title: Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri
films.note: Hedges as the son, another entry in his run of playing kids dealing with pain the rest of society deems unacceptable.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: called his best part of the bunch; also a highlight for Timothée Chalamet.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Chalamet's already signed on for Greta Gerwig's follow-up.

films.title: Burning
films.note: Steven Yeun as the wealthy, unsettling third wheel; Amanda has it high on her year-end list.

films.title: Sorry to Bother You
films.note: Wesley wrote about Yeun here instead of in Burning, calling him a genuine movie star in this one.

films.title: Roma
films.note: Yalitza Aparicio's first-ever screen performance as Cleo, plus Marina de Tavira as the mother she works for.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout
films.note: Tom Cruise's stunt work as its own kind of acting, plus the accidental chemistry with Henry Cavill.

films.title: The Mule
films.note: Sean's reason for staying an Eastwood completist, with a small Bradley Cooper role.

films.title: Support the Girls
films.note: Wesley's favorite performance of the year: Regina Hall, directed by Andrew Bujalski; Haley Lu Richardson also praised.

films.title: Columbus
films.note: where Sean first noticed Haley Lu Richardson.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Bradley Cooper's performance and directing debut get the full lightning-round treatment.

films.title: Yentl
films.note: Barbra Streisand's own directed-and-starring vehicle, raised as the double standard against Cooper.

films.title: Argo
films.note: the comparison point for a director shooting himself a little too lovingly.

films.title: The Town
films.note: argued as the better Affleck movie, and the better Affleck performance.

films.title: Joy
films.note: a small but memorable Cooper role in an otherwise disliked film.

films.title: Silver Linings Playbook
films.note: part of the David O. Russell run that turned Cooper into an awards actor.

films.title: Limitless
films.note: the throwaway movie-star vehicle that first won them over on Cooper.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
films.note: Cooper's Rocket Raccoon voice work, called out for the making-of footage of him fully committing to it.

films.title: Capernaum
films.note: 14-year-old Zain Al Rafeea's performance, Wesley's runner-up pick.

films.title: Wildlife
films.note: Bill Camp and Carey Mulligan's scenes together; Paul Dano's directing debut, written with Zoe Kazan.

films.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
films.note: Amanda's shoutout to Lana Condor, alongside Noah Centineo.

films.title: Shoplifters
films.note: Sakura Ando's performance in the film's back half, Amanda's second pick.

films.title: Game Night
films.note: Jesse Plemons and Rachel McAdams, Sean's 'silly' picks.

films.title: Disobedience
films.note: another McAdams performance raised in the same breath.

films.title: Ocean's Eight
films.note: Anne Hathaway playing on her own reputation for trying too hard, Sean's last pick.

films.title: Ocean's Twelve
films.note: the Julia Roberts self-referential cameo Hathaway's bit is compared to.

To All the Boys I've Loved Before.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
